claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about your child's age.**

How old is the child you're claiming Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child. If you have multiple children, we can go through each one - just start with the first child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh hello, thanks for helping me with this. I'm looking to claim for my son Alex - he's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! You're very welcome - I'm happy to help you with this.

So Alex is 13 years old, which means he's under 16. That's great news because children under 16 automatically qualify in terms of age. Let me move forward with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about Alex's work status.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

At 13, I'd expect the answer is yes - but I just need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all - he's only 13, so he's still at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense! Alex is at school and not working, so that's a yes - he's working less than 24 hours per week. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that are claimed by him personally (not benefits you receive for him). 

At 13 and still at school, I'd expect the answer is yes (he's not receiving these benefits) - but just confirming!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - he doesn't get any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for confirming. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about apprenticeships:

**Is Alex in an apprenticeship?**

Since he's 13 and at school, I'd assume he's not in an apprenticeship - but just to confirm. If he's not in any apprenticeship, we can move straight on!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's just at secondary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's what I thought! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ months)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources (and not claiming certain means-tested benefits)
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, the first option (normally living in the UK) is the relevant one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm afraid none of those apply to me - I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for letting me know. Unfortunately, if you're living abroad and none of the residency paths apply to you (such as being a Crown servant), this affects your eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to give you this news, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** at this time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Summary

**Result: Not Eligible for Child Benefit**

**For Alex:**
- ✅ Age: Qualifies (under 16)
- ✅ Not working full-time
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in his own right
- ✅ Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship
- ❌ **Residency requirement not met** (claimant living abroad)

**Reason:** Although Alex meets all the requirements to be a "qualifying child," Child Benefit requires that **you** (the claimant) normally live in the UK or meet certain special circumstances. Since you're living abroad and don't fall into any of the qualifying categories (such as being a Crown servant), you're unfortunately not eligible to claim.

---

**What you could consider:**

- If your circumstances change and you **return to the UK**, you may then be able to claim Child Benefit
- If you're working for the UK government abroad (Crown servant), you may qualify - it might be worth double-checking if this applies to your situation
- If you have any connection to the EU Settlement Scheme (settled or pre-settled status), there may be options depending on your work/study situation

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify?

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship - all of which would normally qualify him as an eligible child. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and does not qualify under any of the special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, or Crown servant status). Therefore, the claimant cannot receive Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: While Alex meets all the criteria to be a 'qualifying child' for Child Benefit purposes, the claimant does not meet the residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK, or meet specific criteria such as having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being a Crown servant living abroad. Since none of these apply, the claim cannot proceed.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 13 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship - all of which would normally qualify him as an eligible child. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and does not qualify under any of the special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, or Crown servant status). Therefore, the claimant cannot receive Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: While Alex meets all the criteria to be a 'qualifying child' for Child Benefit purposes, the claimant does not meet the residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK, or meet specific criteria such as having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being a Crown servant living abroad. Since none of these apply, the claim cannot proceed.
